official recommended installation sources.

I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the

data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete setup plan for developing .NET MAUI apps on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They can't run commands right now, so they need a reference guide. This is a knowledge/guidance question - I don't need to search a codebase or run commands. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Component | Source | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | Required for iOS SDK, simulators, signing tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Installs compilers & build utilities |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload (Microsoft build of OpenJDK) | Or manually from [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) |

## 2. Install .NET SDK

Download from **[dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)** — get the latest LTS or STS that supports MAUI (currently .NET 8 LTS or .NET 9).

To discover which SDK versions support MAUI:
```
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui#supported-platforms
```

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in Android SDK, Android emulator, iOS/macOS build tools, and the Microsoft OpenJDK.

## 4. Android SDK Management

The workload auto-installs the Android SDK. To discover/manage versions:

```bash
dotnet workload list          # confirm maui is installed
~/.android/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list
```

Accept licenses:
```bash
~/.android/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --licenses
```

## 5. Xcode Setup

After installing Xcode:
```bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
```

## 6. Verify the Installation

```bash
dotnet --info                           # SDK version & runtimes
dotnet workload list                    # maui workload present
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui            # scaffold a project
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (requires Xcode + simulator)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios

# Android (requires emulator or device)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

## 7. Optional but Recommended

- **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight editor with Hot Reload
- **Visual Studio for Mac** is discontinued; VS Code is the official path on macOS now

## 8. Troubleshooting Commands

```bash
dotnet workload repair          # fix broken workload state
dotnet workload update          # update all workloads
xcode-select -p                 # confirm Xcode path
xcrun simctl list devices       # list iOS simulators
```

---

**Key official docs:**  
- [MAUI Get Started](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)  
- [.NET SDK Downloads](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download)  
- [Apple Developer / Xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
